qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: CSRF-exempt backup restore endpoint allows unauthenticated cross-origin arbitrary SQL execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns the backup restore endpoint executing attacker-supplied SQL from an uploaded archive with CSRF protection bypass. The advisory instead describes Zip Slip in `Theme::upload`, where ZIP entry names are extracted without validation and can escape the temporary directory to write arbitrary files. Same application, but different controller, root cause, and exploit primitive.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe deserialization in import endpoint permits attacker-controlled code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is about `Theme::upload` calling `ZipArchive::extractTo()` on user-supplied ZIP content without validating entry names, causing Zip Slip. This finding is an unrelated deserialization RCE pattern and does not identify the theme upload extraction path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. GHSA-xv3r-vr59-95rg is specifically about `modules/Theme/Controllers/Theme.php` extracting theme ZIP archives with `ZipArchive::extractTo()` and failing to validate entry names, allowing Zip Slip and arbitrary file write/RCE. The session findings instead cover CSRF-driven arbitrary SQL execution in backup restore and an unrelated deserialization example. Neither finding targets `Theme::upload` or ZIP entry traversal during theme extraction.
evidence: The advisory names `modules/Theme/Controllers/Theme.php:13-56` and explains that theme upload calls `ZipArchive::extractTo()` with no entry-name validation.

The `csrf_backup_sql_injection` finding instead targets `POST /backend/backup/restore`, where uploaded backup content causes SQL execution with CSRF exposure; this is a different endpoint and exploit primitive from theme Zip Slip.
The `example` finding is unrelated to the theme upload controller and does not involve ZIP entry traversal.
